full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|4.48|> Here we are again.<|6.68|> <|6.68|> Good Friday.<|8.68|> <|9.68|> Years ago, when I was doing my seminary internship<|13.84|> <|15.04|> at Mary Magdalene in Boulder,<|18.16|> <|18.16|> I was the acolyte for the Good Friday service,<|21.08|> <|22.08|> whose job it was to hold up the gospel book<|25.60|> <|26.26|> as the deacon read the Passion.<|28.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Friends, you don't realize truly how long that is until you're holding the book for someone else to read it.<|10.06|> <|10.96|> And of course, I struggle mightily to stand still, so this wasn't easy.<|18.06|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.44|> Bill read in a strong, loud voice<|6.44|> <|7.20|> that un-mic'd would carry throughout the church.<|10.98|> <|12.00|> I stood not even a foot in front of him while he read.<|17.00|> <|17.90|> He basically projected right into my face<|21.96|> <|21.96|> something unthinkable in these days of COVID.<|25.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Every time I hear this story, I think of that day.<|6.80|> <|7.76|> And I've heard the passion countless times in my life, but none have had the impact of that reading.<|16.92|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I literally felt the words coming at me.<|5.12|> <|6.12|> The staccato and violent explosions of breath embodying the crowd's frenzy of crucify him.<|13.60|> <|16.30|> I'm not sure at what point I realized that tears were streaming down my face.<|22.42|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|2.00|> Here we are again. Good Friday.<|7.00|> <|7.00|> Year after year, we repeat the stories of this holiest of weeks.<|15.00|> <|15.00|> Maundy Thursday with its new covenant, new commandment, to love one another.<|23.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Good Friday with the austerity and violence of the cross and Jesus' last moments and last words.<|10.02|> <|11.96|> Easter with its hope, its glory, and its alleluias.<|17.38|> <|19.26|> Their gifts in repetition are necessary.<|23.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> there. Some years it is simply hard to be present to this week. Maybe we're feeling overwhelmed,<|9.60|> <|9.60|> distracted, distant, or even just lazy. Repetition affords us the opportunity to come back when we can be present.<|22.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to put this familiar week and these foundational stories in conversation with our personal lives<|10.52|> <|10.52|> and in conversation with the events of the world around us in real time.<|17.66|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|0.04|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The passion tells us of the abuse of power, the murder of an innocent man, brutality and violence driven by fear and self-interest, angry mobs calling for death.<|22.28|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> There is a great deal that continues to ring true in the details of the passion.<|6.56|> <|7.98|> So much feels relatable to this past year in particular.<|13.92|> <|15.62|> Both the universal and the individual are present in the story of Jesus' final moments.<|25.02|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.50|> This year, hearing the stories of Holy Week as they are in conversation with our present world,<|9.42|> <|10.38|> I keep being drawn to Jesus' words from the cross.<|14.22|> <|15.26|> It is finished.<|16.42|> <|17.84|> It is finished.<|19.36|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But what exactly is finished?<|3.96|> <|5.08|> In John, Jesus is calm and confident and completely in control.<|10.24|> <|10.88|> In John, Jesus carries his own cross.<|14.32|> <|14.80|> He references and fulfills scripture that make him both a king and a priest.<|21.98|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> He redefines family by joining the beloved disciple and his own mother in kinship.<|8.26|> <|9.86|> In John, Jesus' thirst is quenched by a sour wine-soaked sponge delivered to his lips on a branch of hyssop.<|22.16|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|>.<|7.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Hyssop, that same plant used to put the saving blood of the lamb on the Israelites' doorposts in Exodus.<|10.64|> <|11.84|> For John's Jesus, it is finished.<|16.90|> <|17.64|> Means it is perfected and completed and fulfilled.<|21.00|> <|21.62|> Christ has conquered.<|23.28|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Lord.<|1.42|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In my mind this week, this year, reflecting on this statement, I am drawn to thinking instead that this is finished.<|12.66|> <|13.88|> Feels more apt. This body, this part of life, this chapter.<|18.94|> <|20.04|> For there is still so much work to do.<|23.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Abuse of power, the murder of innocent people, brutality and violence driven by fear and self-interest, angry mobs calling for death.<|15.74|> <|16.74|> All of these remain.<|19.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.60|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.04|> I drove on Colfax this week, coming to St. John's,<|5.88|> <|6.26|> and in front of the Fillmore Theater,<|8.96|> <|10.00|> they have Martin Luther King's words literally up in lights right now.<|17.56|> <|19.30|> Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere.<|24.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.36|> There is still so much work to do.<|6.24|> <|8.24|> Yes, it is finished in so many ways.<|13.54|> <|13.90|> All of the ways that give us as followers of the crucified Christ<|19.12|> <|19.12|> the strength and the courage to begin and keep going.<|24.40|> <|24.40|> Amen.<|24.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To continue Jesus' work of bringing about God's kingdom here, now.<|9.20|> <|11.20|> Here we are again.<|12.96|> <|14.42|> Good Friday.<|15.34|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The repetition of this holiest of weeks, and particularly this day, this Good Friday,<|7.70|> <|8.80|> root us in the mystery and the reality of our faith.<|13.58|> <|14.74|> This day and the passion narrative bring us face to face with the sacrifice, the victory, and yes,<|27.34|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> the work still to be done.<|2.80|> <|5.18|> Be well, my friends.<|6.98|> <|8.00|> I miss you.<|8.98|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|2.00|> Here we are again. Good Friday.<|7.00|>
<|7.00|> Year after year, we repeat the stories of this holiest of weeks.<|15.00|>
<|15.00|> Maundy Thursday with its new covenant, new commandment, to love one another.<|23.00|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Good Friday with the austerity and violence of the cross and Jesus' last moments and last words.<|10.02|>
<|11.96|> Easter with its hope, its glory, and its alleluias.<|17.38|>
<|19.26|> Their gifts in repetition are necessary.<|23.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> there. Some years it is simply hard to be present to this week. Maybe we're feeling overwhelmed,<|9.60|>
<|9.60|> distracted, distant, or even just lazy. Repetition affords us the opportunity to come back when we can be present.<|22.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to put this familiar week and these foundational stories in conversation with our personal lives<|10.52|>
<|10.52|> and in conversation with the events of the world around us in real time.<|17.66|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|0.04|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The passion tells us of the abuse of power, the murder of an innocent man, brutality and violence driven by fear and self-interest, angry mobs calling for death.<|22.28|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> There is a great deal that continues to ring true in the details of the passion.<|6.56|>
<|7.98|> So much feels relatable to this past year in particular.<|13.92|>
<|15.62|> Both the universal and the individual are present in the story of Jesus' final moments.<|25.02|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.50|> This year, hearing the stories of Holy Week as they are in conversation with our present world,<|9.42|>
<|10.38|> I keep being drawn to Jesus' words from the cross.<|14.22|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But what exactly is finished?<|3.96|>
<|5.08|> In John, Jesus is calm and confident and completely in control.<|10.24|>
<|10.88|> In John, Jesus carries his own cross.<|14.32|>
<|14.80|> He references and fulfills scripture that make him both a king and a priest.<|21.98|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> He redefines family by joining the beloved disciple and his own mother in kinship.<|8.26|>
<|9.86|> In John, Jesus' thirst is quenched by a sour wine-soaked sponge delivered to his lips on a branch of hyssop.<|22.16|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|>.<|7.00|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Hyssop, that same plant used to put the saving blood of the lamb on the Israelites' doorposts in Exodus.<|10.64|>
<|11.84|> For John's Jesus, it is finished.<|16.90|>
<|17.64|> Means it is perfected and completed and fulfilled.<|21.00|>
<|21.62|> Christ has conquered.<|23.28|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Lord.<|1.42|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In my mind this week, this year, reflecting on this statement, I am drawn to thinking instead that this is finished.<|12.66|>
<|13.88|> Feels more apt. This body, this part of life, this chapter.<|18.94|>
<|20.04|> For there is still so much work to do.<|23.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.60|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.04|> I drove on Colfax this week, coming to St. John's,<|5.88|>
<|6.26|> and in front of the Fillmore Theater,<|8.96|>
<|10.00|> they have Martin Luther King's words literally up in lights right now.<|17.56|>

<|8.24|> Yes, it is finished in so many ways.<|13.54|>
<|13.90|> All of the ways that give us as followers of the crucified Christ<|19.12|>
<|19.12|> the strength and the courage to begin and keep going.<|24.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To continue Jesus' work of bringing about God's kingdom here, now.<|9.20|>
<|11.20|> Here we are again.<|12.96|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The repetition of this holiest of weeks, and particularly this day, this Good Friday,<|7.70|>
